description: This figure presents (S)TEM characterization results of the initial microstructure of Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel. Subfigures a1-a8 are STEM-EDS elemental mapping of Fe, Mn, Al, C, V, and Ni, showing elemental distribution and enrichment of Ni and V corresponding to nanoprecipitates. Subfigure b is a high-resolution TEM (HRTEM) image of the B2/austenite phase interface, with the orientation relationship [011]γ // [111]B2 confirmed by the inset selected area electron diffraction pattern. Subfigure c is an HRTEM image of an individual VC precipitate, with orientation relationship [011]γ // [011]VC verified by the inset diffraction pattern. Subfigure d is a dark-field TEM image captured from the L'1₂ superlattice reflection, showing dispersed L'1₂ nanodomains. Subfigure e is an inverse fast Fourier transform image highlighting L'1₂-type ordered nanodomains. These results confirm the initial microstructure consists of austenite+B2 duplex matrix, plus in-situ precipitated VC nanocarbides and L'1₂ long-range ordered domains in austenite, providing baseline microstructure evidence for subsequent strengthening mechanism analysis.
